inline_comments.message: Is this an override of what's declared in policy/parent? If so, then add a comment why this version is necessary vs what is declared in in parent.
I'm using a mockito as an extension to JUnit 5, mockito-junit-jupiter, which is not available in the parent pom. I believe I could use without this extension if desired.
yes, if possible, let's stick with junit4 & the mockito that's already included.

Is will be great to create the file in UI instead of creating the file and then uploading it. Something like creating tabs in the UI for Model, Configuration & Tosca.
Any plans for that?
question: are all of these thread-safe?  or does it matter?

are we sure that neither the key nor value will contain quotes or other special characters?  Another way to do this would be to copy the relevant key/value pairs into a new Map and then use a (static) StandardCoder to encode the Map into a string - that should work even with special characters
"value" => "default value"
LOGGER.exit
better to use a ValidationResult instead of a Set
use apache IOUtils.toString() instead
we spent a lot of effort eliminating jackson, would prefer not to reintroduce it

can any of the methods in these lines throw an exception if the field is missing or of the wrong type, size, etc.?

similar question in the other files that use JsonObject
should be able to replace these two lines with a call to YamlJsonTranslator::toYaml, eliminating the need for the two methods
use ValidationResult instead
same comment - use apache IOUtils

if the string were decoded into defined data structures, then most of the validation could be done via annotations and then enum class could be eliminated.  It might take more lines, but would probably be clearer and less error prone.

similar comment for the other XxxProcessor classes

maybe use:

  this(xxx, yyy)

that way the junit test for the second constructor will at least verify most of what the no-arg constructor does, too.
if "id" is required, maybe change "Long" to "long"?
trailing spaces
trailing spaces
does the server code verify that this parameters are actually provided?
prefer to use assertThat(xxx).contains(yyy)

maybe use try-with-resources to ensure input streams are closed

same comment with the other input streams in this and other Test files
why bother catching the exception instead of just allowing it to propagate up?
same question
same comment - avoid jackson
I think the convention is that all test methods should begin with "test".  (Might be a sonar issue, not sure.)
can use StandardYamlCoder instead

use assertThat(xxx).containsKey(yyy)

same comment below
sonar: remove println
same comment as before - why bother catching the exception?
or the JDK's Files.readString() for files (as the argument in line 58 indicates).
I thought metadata section that includes the "policy-id" was mandatory .., not sure if it applies in the context of how this file is used, but wanted to bring it up to your attention

I also think this would be even better. To the existing UI, adding 2 more tabs, one for config and other for tosca_template. Then the submission will result in using the APexCliToscaEditor to create a ToscaPolicy. Uploading them separately instead seems to be more tedious requiring more validations.

Use assertThatThrownBy..isInstanceOf..hasMessage..
Same comment below.

Already there is code in APEX to read the configuration file from a file path, parse and validate it etc. We could reuse those (maybe some modifications might be needed). I think this UI part could be more simpler reusing more of what is already there.

I completely agree. Could you please point me out where I can find this code?

to avoid having an undesirable initial value. I prefer to have the null if nothing is set. The endpoint that receives this information should validate it.

yes, there is a validation process for that
Yes, in the future we are planing to have some interface to handle those files, probably for the next release.

just to fail the test with a user friendly message, but checking now, the default exception message is already enough.

I've based the APEX policy TOSCA conversion code based on the apex-pdp/auth/cli-editor conversion code, and there this is not being considered. The ones I'm validating are: tosca_definitions_version, topology_template, topology_template->policies, topology_template->policies[0]["anyPolicyName"]->properties
FYI: it's OK to make this static; it's thread safe
might be able to just encode(uploadConfigParam), then could eliminate the need for the getValue() method in UploadPluginConfigParameters
it has various sub classes that may or may not be relevant.  Point is, there is already a class for capturing one or more error messages from validation methods
no, you're correct - Files.readString() won't work with an InputStream
could combine these two lines using entry set iterator instead of getting key and then value.  Or if order doesn't matter, then could just use a values iterator.
seem to be repeating this kind of code.  wonder if there's a way to avoid that using functional programming?  though it might not be much shorter, so maybe not worth it
thx for explaining

This class is exactly doing what ApexCliToscaEditor class is doing. Don't see any point in validating the structure of config and tosca_template files and then doing this conversion here. The validations and conversions should be done in APEX repo itself, which it already is doing, gui repo should be just utilising them.
perhaps something should be moved to policy-common and shared?
I think that's a good idea, this class may be of benefit for other functionality.

@Ajith, you mean this class org.onap.policy.apex.auth.clieditor.tosca.ApexCliToscaEditorMain, from apex-pdp/auth/cli-editor module?
I can try to reuse it and also update the code to do include this extra validations I'm doing. Is that the idea?
Done

I believe that the encode will bring me more information then the one I need (the name attribute for instance) and will not parse the uploadConfigParam attribute to a PolicyUploadPluginConfigKey::getKey.
If the encode cannot reproduce this, I prefer to leave this way.
Ok, I will need to move those validation/processing methods to the ApexEditorCli code and there I will implement using the ValidationResult.
Could not find a values iterator, so I'm using the entrySet.
Did in a more functional way but had to generalize some error messages. This logic will be moved to the ApexEditorCli, so there I can think in a better way to improve the messages.

how about:
  1) use standardCoder.convert() to convert it to a map
  2) remove the "name" field
  3) use standardCoder.encode() to encode the map into a string

Can use the SerializedName annotation in the Params class to specify field renaming.

I think that would simplify this method, eliminate the need for the "type" field in the enum, and also eliminate the need for the getValue method in the Params class.
yeah, it's fine to add the shared class in a later review
oh, right, this is a JsonObject, not a map.  Yeah, entrySet sounds good
This component should be converted to use policy endpoints from policy-common

We should just work with the full TOSCA file from now on, the old APEX JSON file should be subsumed into the overall model and should vanish.

So rather than uploading a template and a config file, there should be a single file.
Please explain the need for this endpoint
Not sure what this is.

We seem to have parallel processing for the config here. I'm not sure if we need this, we may be able to reuse existing stuff in the Policy Framework for this.

We already have a lot of support for TOSCA handling in policy-models
Same comment.
A lot of overlap with the policy models TOSCA handling.
